title: Call of Duty®: Black Ops II
Call of Duty®: Black Ops II

notes:
The game still doesn't run on Proton, however, on vanilla wine and only up to the 4.18.x kernel, starting from the 4.19+ kernel the game does not start anymore.
Source: https://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=46472

For Nvidia graphics cards should be ensured that in the X-server settings "V sync" and "Allow Flipping" is off, otherwise the game does not start, as it looks at AMD I can not say. The system and the Wine version must be compatible with E-Sync, otherwise Black Ops II crashes within 35 minutes of playtime (seems to be a memory overhead inside the engine).

The Game runs around 50 ~ 90FPS both on Multiplayer and Zombies (played Mob of the Dead and TranZit aswell to be sure) with minor stuttering that does not affect the game experience, you guys may get some better performance out of it.

The only disadvantage is that you cannot be the lobby-host (in zombie mode, even in a single-player game) what makes the game unplayable, it acts like you play with an extremely high ping with zombies bouncing back and forth and you can barely move at all. So ask a friend who invites you to a round of zombies. :)

You have to create a x86 wineprefix, set win7, install steam in iside of it, launch steam with this command line argument "-steamos" to get your friendlist working and launch the game in a virtual desktop otherwise it will freeze your system completly when you launch Black Ops II.
